title: Example API — Architecture
subtitle: A worked example shipped with the code-map skill (Bun + Hono API + background worker, one Postgres).
summary: A two-process system: a synchronous HTTP API and a background worker sharing one database. Use this file as a template for the shape of a real data.json.

nodes.label: Dashboard SPA
nodes.description: Browser frontend that calls the API with a session token and renders the dashboard.
decisions: Talks only to the public API; never to the worker or DB directly.

nodes.label: API server
nodes.description: Public HTTP service. Mounts CORS, security headers, logging, docs, and the /api/v1 routes.
decisions: Holds no encryption key; secret handling is delegated to the worker.

nodes.label: Middleware
nodes.description: Request logging, rate limiting, authentication, and the global error handler.
decisions: Rate limiting fails open if the limiter store is down, to avoid a full outage.

nodes.label: v1 routes
nodes.description: Mounts the versioned route modules and wires each to its controller + OpenAPI metadata.
decisions: Each route file owns its own OpenAPI metadata so generated SDKs stay accurate.

nodes.label: Services
nodes.description: Where business decisions live; controllers stay thin and call into these.
decisions: Org scoping is explicit — services take an organizationId from auth context.

nodes.label: Repositories
nodes.description: Isolate Prisma queries, raw SQL, public mapping, and tenant scoping.
decisions: Raw SQL is used where Prisma can't express queue locking or fast summaries.

nodes.label: Postgres
nodes.description: Stores identity, org resources, the job queue, and domain data.
decisions: Jobs are locked with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED for multi-worker safety.

nodes.label: Scheduler
nodes.description: Cron tasks that enqueue durable jobs; they do no work themselves.
decisions: Schedules are UTC for consistent day/month boundaries.

nodes.label: Job runner
nodes.description: Polls the queue, locks one job, dispatches by type, and requeues or fails on error.
decisions: Unknown job types fail explicitly to protect against silent queue drift.

nodes.label: External APIs
nodes.description: Third-party services the worker and services call out to.
decisions: Provider error classes preserve 401/403/429 semantics for handlers.

flows.label: Read request
flows.title: A dashboard read, end to end
steps: Middleware logs, rate-limits, and authenticates the request.
The router resolves the path and calls the controller.
A service aggregates data, scoped to the caller's org.
Repositories run tenant-scoped queries against Postgres.
decisions: Reads are synchronous and org-scoped; heavy work is pushed to the worker.

stages.title: Dashboard SPA
stages.desc: Browser issues the fetch with a session token.

stages.title: Service
stages.desc: Aggregates the summary, scoped to the org.

stages.title: Response
stages.desc: Serialized DTO returns to the browser.

notes.text: Two-process model: synchronous API + durable background worker, sharing one Postgres.
This is an example input. Replace it with a scan of your own repo — every node's files[] must point at real paths.
